title: TCP Connector Options
text: TCP Connector Options You can assign connector options by setting USYS$TCP_PARAMS in the [DRIVER_SETTINGS] section of the assignment file. USYS$TCP_PARAMS = { ub = MaximumPacketSize } { , %/ sb = MaximumSendBufferKB } { , %/ rb = MaximumReceiveBufferKB } { , %/ n =0 | 1 } { , nl } { , %/ prf =4 | 6 } { , %/ ql = QueueLength { , %/ ka } Options ka (Keep Alive) n (Nagling) nl (No Linger) prf (Version Preference) ql (Queue Length) rb (Receive Buffer) sb (Send Buffer) ub (Uniface buffer) Description TCP connector options enable you to tune the TCP connector performance. The TCP connector creates the physical connection for both TCP/IP and TLS connections. When you set connector options for the TCP connector, these settings stay in effect for all TCP and TLS connections. [DRIVER_SETTINGS] USYS$TCP_PARAMS = ub=4096, sb=16, n=1, nl Copy Related Links [DRIVER_SETTINGS] Use the [DRIVER_SETTINGS] section of the assignment file to specify the database and component engine connectors used, and their parameter options. The options available depend on the connector in use. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
